title: China urges reversal of UNIFIL withdrawal from Lebanon amid escalating Israeli attacks
summary: China's UN Ambassador expressed deep concern about Lebanon, calling for re-examination of the UNIFIL withdrawal decision as peacekeepers face casualties and Israeli attacks kill 2,618 since March.
contents:
## China Pushes to Reverse UNIFIL Withdrawal

### Chinese Position
China's UN Ambassador Fu Cong, speaking on May 2, 2026, as Beijing assumed the Security Council's rotating presidency for May, called for re-examining the decision to terminate UNIFIL's mandate in Lebanon. Fu stated the "overwhelming majority of the Security Council" agreed the timing for withdrawal appeared wrong.

### Situation on Ground
Fu described the current state as merely a "lesser fire" rather than a genuine ceasefire. According to Lebanese authorities, Israeli attacks since March 2 have killed 2,618 people and forced more than one million to flee their homes. At least six peacekeepers have died and many others sustained injuries since the Israeli offensive began.

### UNIFIL Background
The UN Interim Force in Lebanon, created after Israel's 1978 invasion, maintained 10,800 international peacekeepers. The Security Council unanimously resolved in 2025 to begin withdrawing the force by December 2026. UNIFIL performed essential duties including clearing explosive ordnance and escorting logistics convoys.

### Broader Context
UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres condemned attacks on peacekeepers. The debate over UNIFIL's future intensified as Lebanon's parliament speaker Nabih Berri accused Israel of using the ceasefire as cover to intensify attacks, with 12 people killed on May 1 alone in southern Lebanon.
